CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-23-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AUTO-CHLOR SYSTEM OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

Defendant Auto-Chlor System of Northern California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied in full.

Defendant argues this case does not belong in court because plaintiff cannot establish an actual, qualifying FEHA disability which causes the first, second, third, and fourth causes of action to fail. However, the court finds that plaintiff does establish a "physical disability, mental disability, or medical condition" pursuant to the statutory definition. (Gov. Code, section 12920.) The physiological condition or impairment must be such that it "limits a major life activity" meaning it "makes the achievements of the major life activity difficult." (Gov. Code, section 12926 subd. (m)(1)(B)(ii).) "Major life activities" shall be broadly construed. (Id. at subd. (m)(1)(B)(iii).) The definition of "major life activities" includes breathing. (County of Fresno v. Fair Employment & Housing Comm'n (1992) 226 Cal.App.3d 1541, 1548 [hypersensitivity to tobacco smoke was a FEHA "physical disability" because it interfered with a major activity of breathing].)

Plaintiff presents evidence showing he has suffered from pneumonia since he was a child. (Gonzalez Decl., 5 [symptoms include shortness of breath].) When he had his flare up in August 2019, he was admitted to the hospital and called his supervisor, Mr. Phillips, to let him know about his pneumonia and that we would be unable to come into work. (Otkupman Decl, Gonzalez Depo. 27:3-14, 29:25-30:4.) Plaintiff provided defendant with doctors' notes after he returned to work. (Id.) Mr. Phillips testified that he received a call from plaintiff letting him know about the illness. (Otkupman Decl, Phillips Depo. 76:11-20.) Defendant narrowly focuses on the doctors' notes which indicate plaintiff had a cough. (Gonzalez Decl., Ex. 3 and 4.) However, the note also lists "medical reasons", which could include the plaintiff's pneumonia symptoms of shortness of breath, shallow breathing, chest pain, cough, allergic reaction, weakness, fatigue, and inability to breathe. (Gonzalez Decl., 5.) Thus, plaintiff presents evidence to establish he is a protected individual with an actual and qualifying disability.

Likewise, summary adjudication as to the fifth and sixth causes of action is denied. Defendant again argues plaintiff does not have an actual, qualifying disability. As discussed above, the court disagrees. Plaintiff properly pleads a statutory violation for wrongful termination.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
